eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket pins the United Kingdom at 29.5% and Austria at 26.5% implied probability for last place in the Eurovision 2026 Grand Final, driven by lackluster reactions to their first and second rehearsal clips released over the past week at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle. LOOK MUM NO COMPUTER's quirky, gadget-filled "Eins, Zwei, Drei" has sparked fears of televote rejection despite chaotic staging energy, while host nation Austria's COSMÓ and German-language "Tanzschein" drew backlash as a perceived downgrade, labeled niche or gimmicky by fans and reactors. Germany and Belgium trail at 8.5% each amid broader field strength from frontrunners like Finland; jury show outcomes and Saturday's May 16 final televote could swing this tight race, with Big Five auto-qualification heightening scrutiny on performance polish.

markets.question: Will OpenAI announce a computer (Laptop/Desktop) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to "Yes" if the listed type of consumer hardware product is publicly announced by OpenAI by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The announcement of a qualifying consumer hardware product within the market timeframe will suffice, regardless of whether or when the product is released.

If a device fulfills multiple device categories, then all relevant categories will resolve to "Yes".

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI's early 2026 product cadence has centered on software advancements, including the GPT-5.5 model for agentic tasks and real-world intelligence, GPT-Realtime-2 for production voice agents, ChatGPT Images 2.0, and enterprise platforms like Frontier and workspace agents, with no consumer hardware announced to date. Trader sentiment reflects persistent rumors of a hardware pivot, fueled by January leaks on Jony Ive-partnered "Sweet Pea" AI earbuds targeting a second-half reveal, executive confirmations of 2026 devices, and Foxconn production reports. A $122 billion funding round bolsters compute infrastructure ambitions amid rivalry with Apple and Meta in wearables, with upcoming developer events or Sam Altman updates as pivotal catalysts before year-end.

markets.description:

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 76% implied probability to AI coding platform Cursor being acquired before 2027, propelled by SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a $60 billion option to buy its parent Anysphere after Microsoft declined amid intensifying competition for developer tools and compute resources. Caesars Entertainment follows at 73%, buoyed by broader M&A momentum in gaming and hospitality, while biotech firm Viking Therapeutics sits at 58% on acquisition speculation. Recent sector catalysts include iRobot's January court-approved sale to Picea Robotics, Google's $32 billion Wiz deal, and SpaceX's Q1 xAI purchase, signaling aggressive AI consolidation. Watch Q2 earnings from GitLab and Zoom for M&A signals, plus ongoing OpenAI and Anthropic talks, though regulatory hurdles and deal slips remain key risks in this fast-evolving landscape.

markets.question: Racing Club de Lens vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game between Racing Club de Lens and Paris Saint-Germain FC, scheduled for May 13 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Racing Club de Lens and Paris Saint-Germain FC combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the intensifying Ligue 1 title race, Paris Saint-Germain hold a narrow four-point lead over second-placed RC Lens after Round 30, with five matches left including this rescheduled Week 29 fixture at Stade Bollaert-Delelis on May 13. Lens, transformed into genuine contenders under Pierre Sage, recently dropped points in a 3-3 draw at Brest amid defensive injury woes—Jonathan Gradit (lower leg fracture), Régis Gurtner (hamstring), and Kyllian Antonio (foot) sidelined—exposing center-back depth issues. PSG, dominant with a seven-match unbeaten streak away, welcome back Fabián Ruiz from knee trouble but monitor Vitinha's heel inflammation; they previously beat Lens 2-0 earlier this season in a head-to-head favoring Les Parisiens over seven straight meetings. Home form and title pressure give Lens upset potential despite PSG's superior squad depth and European rest advantages.

markets.question: Will Arsenal finish in 2nd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding five-point lead atop the Premier League table (79 points from 36 games, +42 goal difference) over Manchester City (74 points from 35, +40 GD) drives trader consensus assigning City an 86% implied probability of finishing 2nd, with third-placed Manchester United distant at 65 points from 36 games. Arsenal solidified their buffer via a gritty 1-0 win at West Ham on May 10 despite Ben White's knee injury, following City's 3-0 rout of Brentford on May 9 that kept faint title hopes alive. A City 1st-place finish—yielding Arsenal 2nd at 13.5% odds—hinges on City winning today's game in hand versus Crystal Palace plus their final two, while Arsenal stumbles in home clashes starting with Burnley on May 18.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors the Partido Liberal (PL) at 77% implied probability to win the most seats among the 27 Senate seats contested on October 4, 2026, across nine states, reflecting the party's strong organizational strength, high-profile candidates aligned with former President Jair Bolsonaro, and robust right-wing voter mobilization. Recent April polls, including AtlasIntel/Bloomberg and Datafolha surveys, show Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) statistically tied or leading President Lula in simulated presidential runoffs, signaling broad momentum for PL amid Lula's rising but vulnerable approval ratings. Local polls reinforce this, with PL frontrunners like José Medeiros in Mato Grosso and Carol de Toni in Santa Catarina; Republicanos trails at 9.2% due to evangelical base consolidation, while centrists MDB and PSD lag amid fragmented opposition. Key risks include turnout shifts or coalition shifts before candidate filings close in August.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CO-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CO-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Diana DeGette's longstanding hold on Colorado's 1st Congressional District, a reliably Democratic stronghold in urban Denver with voter registration favoring Democrats by wide margins, underpins trader consensus pricing the Democratic Party nominee at 92.5% to win the November 3 general election. DeGette's past landslide victories—often exceeding 40-point margins—combined with weak Republican opposition solidify this position, despite her facing a spirited Democratic primary challenge from Melat Kiros and Wanda James ahead of the June 30 vote, where she narrowly qualified in March. Scenarios that could shift odds include a major scandal engulfing the Democratic nominee post-primary, an unexpectedly strong GOP recruit, or a national Republican wave boosting turnout in battleground races.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 36°C or higher on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Highest temperature in Karachi on May 14?

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) forecasts position 36°C or higher as the market-implied frontrunner at 65.5% for Karachi's May 14 maximum temperature, projecting daytime highs of 35–37°C under persistent hot, dry continental airflow and high-pressure subsidence over Sindh. This trader consensus reflects recent moderation from an early-May heatwave peaking at 44.1°C on May 4—driven by strengthened southerly sea breezes—but with observations rebounding to 34°C on May 12 amid clear skies and intense solar insolation typical of pre-monsoon conditions. AccuWeather aligns with 38–39°C potential, though humidity-elevated heat index could exceed 45°C. Uncertainties include afternoon gusts capping intensity; PMD airport readings will resolve, with updates expected throughout May 14.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open until September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET to allow for confirmation. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment prices minimal risk of major bank failures by June 30 amid a stabilized U.S. banking sector, bolstered by post-2023 regulatory enhancements like higher capital requirements and liquidity buffers. The FDIC's orderly resolutions of two small institutions—Metropolitan Capital Bank & Trust ($261 million assets) on January 30 and Community Bank & Trust-West Georgia on May 1—incurred limited losses to the Deposit Insurance Fund, signaling contained vulnerabilities without contagion. Commercial real estate exposure remains a key pressure point, with office loan delinquencies over 12% and $100 billion in CMBS maturities looming this year, though large banks maintain capital ratios well above supervisory stress thresholds. The Federal Reserve's May 2026 Financial Stability Report notes reduced uninsured deposit reliance. Watch Q2 earnings, June FOMC policy signals, and annual stress test results for shifts.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will João Roma win the 2026 Bahia gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Bahia gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Governor Jerônimo Rodrigues holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the October 4, 2026, Bahia gubernatorial election amid polls showing a statistical tie with challenger ACM Neto, reflecting recent tightening from earlier wide leads for Neto. Late April Quaest and early May Veritá surveys depict Jerônimo gaining ground to 37-42% in valid votes versus Neto's 41-56%, bolstered by PT's dominance in 356 of 417 municipalities and 51% approval for re-election per Quaest. Neto leverages frustration with the administration and his Salvador base, recently challenging Jerônimo to debate while touring the interior. A first-round majority remains elusive, with a potential runoff on October 25 hinging on undecideds, turnout in battleground areas, and national coattails.

England's 12% reflects steady Nations League and qualifier form under consistent leadership, but the race stays tight amid a deep 48-team field including powerhouses like Brazil and defending champions Argentina, both around 9%, with no major injuries or disruptions in the past month as club seasons conclude. The expanded format's group stage draw and knockout volatility amplify upset potential across elite squads.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors self-funding businessman Perry Johnson at 53% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, 2026, edging U.S. Rep. John James at 40.5%, amid dueling petition signature challenges filed last week with the Michigan Bureau of Elections alleging invalid and fraudulent signatures for both frontrunners—echoing Johnson's 2022 disqualification over thousands of bogus signatures. April polls, including Glengariff Group and OnMessage internals, showed James leading 37-41% to Johnson's 18-20%, but traders appear discounting the challenges while betting on Johnson's heavy ad spending and 2022 name recognition against James' congressional profile and establishment backing. Mike Cox trails at 7.6% on former attorney general experience, with resolution of challenges potentially tipping the closely contested field.

markets.question: Will Prosperous Armenia win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Armenia on June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Armenian National Assembly. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Civil Contract's commanding 93.5% implied probability as the leading party in Armenia's June 7 parliamentary election reflects its persistent dominance in recent polls, including EVN Report's early May survey showing 56% support amid opposition fragmentation under proportional representation rules requiring 4-8% thresholds. The incumbent party's position strengthened with the third-wave Armenian Election Study data indicating rising approval for Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's government, bolstered by its pivot toward EU integration and framing opponents as a pro-war bloc during the campaign launch last week. Arrests of Strong Armenia figures in mid-April further weakened the main challenger at 17%. Upsets could arise from a late scandal, Azerbaijan border escalation, or consolidated opposition turnout surge, though structural fragmentation favors the frontrunner securing the most seats.

markets.question: New York Red Bulls vs. Columbus Crew: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between New York Red Bulls and Columbus Crew, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both New York Red Bulls and Columbus Crew each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: New York Red Bulls vs. Columbus Crew - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Red Bulls hold seventh place in the MLS Eastern Conference standings with 11 points from seven matches (3-2-2, GD -4), buoyed by solid home form including three wins in their last six at Red Bull Arena, while Columbus Crew sit 11th on six points (1-3-3, GD -1) amid road woes with three losses in six away games. Recent Matchday 7 results saw Crew draw goalless with Chicago and lose 0-1 to Nashville earlier, contrasting Red Bulls' mixed bag including a home win over FC Cincinnati. Key absences shape lineups: Red Bulls without Justin Che (hamstring, late April return) and AJ Marcucci (knee), Cameron Harper questionable (hamstring); Crew missing Mohamed Farsi (sports hernia). Even head-to-head record (NYRB 28W-30L-15D) points to a tight Eastern Conference battle on May 13.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk and Sam Altman settle?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman in Musk v. Altman et al., Case No. 4:24-cv-04722-YGR (U.S. District Court, Northern District of California), are settled by 11:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A settlement refers to an agreement between Elon Musk and Sam Altman that resolves all claims between them in this case, whether through a formal settlement agreement, stipulated dismissal, or other court-recognized resolution indicating that the claims between these two parties have been resolved. Announcements of such a settlement will qualify, regardless of whether final paperwork is completed or court approval occurs after the deadline.

Settlements involving other parties (e.g., OpenAI or any additional defendants) will not count unless they also resolve all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman.

If all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman are resolved through a final court judgment (e.g., trial verdict or dispositive ruling), or through a unilateral dismissal of claims not constituting a settlement, before any qualifying settlement occurs, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. court system (including filings, orders, or docket updates). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 79% implied probability against Elon Musk and Sam Altman settling their OpenAI lawsuit, driven by the high-stakes federal trial now in its second week as of May 13, 2026. Musk's last-ditch settlement overture via email two days before trial began on May 4 was rebuffed, followed by OpenAI's disclosure of "ominous" texts from Musk to Altman and co-founder Greg Brockman, escalating tensions. Sam Altman's Day 10 testimony accused Musk of seeking dynastic control over OpenAI—proposing it pass to his children—while Musk's team challenged Altman's credibility amid the rift over OpenAI's nonprofit-to-for-profit pivot and Microsoft ties. With xAI challenging OpenAI's dominance, upcoming witness testimonies and closing arguments signal traders expect the case to proceed to verdict rather than reconcile.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona lead La Liga standings by 11 points over Real Madrid entering matchday 37, hosting fifth-placed Real Betis at Spotify Camp Nou on May 17 in a fixture with title implications for the hosts and European qualification stakes for the visitors. Barca's dominance is tempered by an injury crisis, with star winger Lamine Yamal sidelined for the season via hamstring tear, though Raphinha and Andreas Christensen recently resumed full training ahead of their May 3 Osasuna clash; doubts linger over Christensen's partial ACL recovery. Betis boast solid away form after stunning Real Madrid in late April but face absences including defender Marc Bartra (foot, late May) and Junior Firpo (muscle). Historical head-to-head favors Barcelona (30 wins in 46 meetings), with home advantage and superior goal difference underscoring trader focus on squad depth and motivation.

markets.question: Will Choo Kyung-ho win the 2026 Daegu mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Daegu mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors People Power Party Rep. Choo Kyung-ho at 71% implied probability to win the June 3 Daegu mayoral election in the conservative stronghold, where no Democratic Party candidate has prevailed, despite recent polls showing a neck-and-neck race with ruling party contender and former Prime Minister Kim Boo-kyum at around 40% each within the margin of error. Choo secured PPP nomination on April 26 after a multi-candidate primary, leveraging his three-term lawmaker status from Daegu Dalseong, while Kim was selected unopposed in early April with pledges for economic revival. Early May surveys from JTBC and MBC highlight tight contests driven by unaffiliated voters, but historical turnout patterns and party base loyalty position Choo ahead, with candidate registration opening this week potentially influencing final momentum.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Keir Starmer faces acute pressure at the upcoming Prime Minister's Questions on May 20 following Labour's historic losses in the May 7 local elections, where Reform UK made major gains at the party's expense, sparking a leadership revolt with over 80 MPs publicly urging his resignation and several ministers quitting. Starmer has defiantly told his cabinet he will not step down, amid scandals involving security vetting and peerages. Today's King's Speech outlined the government's legislative agenda, providing potential talking points, but opposition leaders like Kemi Badenoch and Stephen Flynn are poised to grill him on electoral fallout, party divisions, and governance record, shaping a combative session likely focused on defense and deflection.

markets.question: Will Trevor Merrell advance from the CA-04 primary election?
markets.description:
The California primary is currently scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

This market will resolve "Yes" if the listed candidate advances from the primary to contest the seat for California's 4th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If no nominees are announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

Any replacement of the nominees before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Mike Thompson faces a competitive Democratic primary challenge from well-funded newcomer Eric Jones in California's 4th Congressional District top-two primary on June 2, with the top two vote-getters advancing to November regardless of party. Recent Federal Election Commission filings through mid-April show Jones slightly edging Thompson in fundraising—$3.25 million versus $2.9 million—bolstered by tech and finance donors, while Thompson draws from PACs and endorsements like Gov. Gavin Newsom's. The district, redrawn by Proposition 50 last November to incorporate conservative Yuba, Sutter, and Colusa counties, remains Solid Democratic per Cook Political Report, but a fragmented Republican field of six candidates, led by Ray Riehle's $108,000 haul, risks vote-splitting. No public polls exist, leaving trader consensus focused on early/mail voting turnout ahead of election day.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chengdu be 13°C or below on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/chengdu/ZUUU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecasts from the China Meteorological Administration and global models like ECMWF project Chengdu's highest temperature on May 15 near 24°C, driving trader consensus toward 21–23°C outcomes with 23°C or higher at a modest 29.5% implied probability. This reflects a sharp drop from recent highs of 28–29°C, caused by an advancing low-pressure system bringing overcast skies, 60–70% rain chances, and showers that suppress diurnal heating through reduced solar insolation and high humidity. Differentiating factors include morning precipitation timing—persistent rain favors 21–22°C, while afternoon clearing could push toward 23°C+—amid model spreads of 23–26°C and historical rainy-day averages 3°C below May's 28°C norm. Evening forecast updates from CMA may shift sentiment before official station resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) forecasts mainly hot and dry conditions over Karachi through May 18, with daytime highs around 38°C under a persistent high-pressure ridge that inhibits cloud development and convection. After peaking at 44°C earlier this month—the city's hottest since 2018 amid a deadly regional heatwave—temperatures have eased but exceed May's climatological average of 35°C, influenced by continental heating and weak Arabian Sea moisture influx. Ensemble models indicate clear skies and southerly winds moderating May 15's peak to near 36°C, though sea breeze variability adds uncertainty. Resolution hinges on PMD measurements at Jinnah International Airport, with daily bulletins providing key updates for traders.

markets.question: Will ChatGPT be #1 Free App in the US Apple App Store on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the iOS app, ranked #1 in the United States on the iPhone Apple App Store's overall Top Charts under "Free Apps", as of 12:00 PM ET on the specified date.

To find the overall chart, click "Apps" at the bottom of the US iOS App Store app, scroll down to "Top Free Apps" and click "See All". Then under "Free Apps" in the "Top Charts" section, you'll see the list that will be used as the resolution source to this market (https://apps.apple.com/us/charts/iphone).

markets.question: Will there be at least 1900 measles cases in the U.S. by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

events.title: Measles cases in U.S. by May 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: As of May 7, 2026, the CDC reports 1,842 confirmed measles cases in the United States this year—reported by 39 jurisdictions—with weekly incidence by rash onset declining sharply to just 7 cases for the week starting May 3, down from peaks exceeding 100 earlier in spring. Over 93% of cases are outbreak-associated, linked to 25 new 2026 outbreaks, primarily among unvaccinated individuals (92% of cases) in under-immunized communities where MMR coverage falls below the 95% herd immunity threshold. Hospitalizations affect 6% of patients, with no fatalities. Trader sentiment reflects this slowdown amid containment efforts, though high transmissibility (R0 of 12-18) sustains uncertainty; watch CDC's next weekly surveillance update around May 14 for cases with onset through May 31 resolution.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-16 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-16 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors the Democratic Party at 94.5% implied probability for the NY-16 House general election winner, driven by incumbent Rep. George Latimer's commanding position in this safely Democratic district spanning southern Westchester County and parts of the Bronx. Latimer cruised to victory in 2024 over his Republican opponent and secured his party's line for re-election in April 2026 ahead of the June 23 Democratic primary, facing negligible intraparty competition. Rated Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report, the seat aligns with historical base rates for incumbent retention in D+leaning districts lacking a credible GOP challenger. Upsets could arise from a late high-profile Republican recruit, a Latimer scandal, depressed Democratic turnout, or a national midterm wave, though these remain remote absent major shifts before November 3.

Forecasters rate the race Likely or Solid Democratic, citing the district's leftward shift via Kamala Harris's solid 2024 presidential performance and lack of credible GOP opposition from Deschutes County Commissioner Patti Adair and attorney Jonathan Lockwood. The May 19 Republican primary looms, but traders anticipate no major shift absent polling surprises or late developments.

markets.question: Will Jerome Powell depart as Fed Chair between June 20 and June 26?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date range during which Jerome Powell vacates his role as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of Powell's resignation or firing will not alone qualify.

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires May 15, driving trader consensus to 95% odds of his departure in the May 15–22 window, as he confirmed in late April he will step aside at term's end while remaining a Board of Governors member until 2028. President Trump's nominee Kevin Warsh advanced through Senate Banking Committee votes last week and secured Board confirmation in a 51-45 tally on May 12, positioning him for a full Senate vote on the chairmanship as early as this week. This smooth confirmation path reflects historical precedent for term-limited chairs yielding promptly. Delays from holds, procedural snags, or recessions could extend Powell's acting role, though recent momentum makes such scenarios unlikely.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

events.title: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93.9% implied probability against the UK designating Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation by June 30, driven by procedural hurdles in proscribing state-backed entities under the Terrorism Act 2000. Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged new legislation in April 2026 to enable such bans following the EU's February designation of the IRGC, but the bill faces delayed introduction—potentially via today's King's Speech—with parliamentary stages unlikely to conclude before the deadline. Ongoing sanctions target IRGC activities amid national security threats, yet cross-party calls and Early Day Motions have yielded no action, underscoring slow legislative timelines as the dominant factor. Late-breaking diplomatic shifts or expedited votes remain possible but improbable.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - May 13, 12:15PM-12:20PM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will CZ post 0-19 posts from May 15 to May 22, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between May 15, 12:00 PM ET and May 22, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

events.title: CZ # posts May 15 - May 22, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between May 15, 12:00 PM ET and May 22, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors CZ posting 20-39 times on X from May 15 to May 22 at 50% implied probability, aligning with his historical average of 25-32 posts per week observed in prior similar markets and calm periods. The <20 outcome trails closely at 38.5%, driven by a recent slowdown to just three posts in the May 6-13 window—including a reply to PancakeSwap, sharing a CoinDesk article on potential Binance.US revival, and boosting a Crypto Banter interview teaser—which signals subdued activity amid steady crypto market conditions. Higher ranges like 40-59 at 7% reflect infrequent spikes from events such as major announcements or volatility; traders monitor for book promotions or BNB Chain updates that could elevate volume before resolution.

markets.question: Will "Gold" be said during the next episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast?
markets.description:
The Lemonade Stand Podcast is scheduled to release episodes every Wednesday. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the next released episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old episodes or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the next episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast posted on YouTube. Any video posted to the channel https://www.youtube.com/@LemonadeStandPodcast with the words "Lemonade Stand" in the title will qualify.

If no such episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast is aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

Absent a teaser from @thelemonadecast as of May 12, odds hinge on news momentum; monitor for Monday hints before Wednesday's YouTube drop.

markets.question: James Harden: Rebounds O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 13 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if James Harden records more than 4.5 total rebounds during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if James Harden records 4.5 rebounds or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Cavaliers vs. Pistons
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 13 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Cavaliers win, the market will resolve to "Cavaliers".
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Eastern Conference Semifinals series between the top-seeded Detroit Pistons and No. 4 Cleveland Cavaliers stands tied 2-2 heading into pivotal Game 5 at Little Caesars Arena, where Detroit's league-best 35-10 home record provides a clear edge amid Cleveland's 0-2 road mark in the playoffs. Cavaliers gained momentum from back-to-back home wins, including Donovan Mitchell's record-tying 39 second-half points in Monday's 112-103 Game 4 triumph, bolstered by James Harden's clutch scoring and Evan Mobley-Jarrett Allen's frontcourt dominance. Pistons counter with Cade Cunningham's playoff-leading points and assists but face depth issues: Caris LeVert (heel), Kevin Huerter (adductor), and Duncan Robinson listed questionable on the injury report, while Cleveland enters fully healthy.

events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between Odisha FC and Punjab FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Odisha FC holds a slim edge as home side at Kalinga Stadium, but trader consensus prices reflect Punjab FC's stronger recent form offsetting that advantage in a relegation-threatened clash for the Juggernauts. Punjab sit 7th with 14 points from eight matches (4W-2D-2L), fresh off a 3-0 dismantling of Inter Kashi on April 20, showcasing solid defense and counter-attacks, while Odisha languish 11th on six points from eight (1W-3D-4L), stung by a stoppage-time loss to Kerala Blasters on April 23 and a 1-1 draw with Mohammedan. Their February 1-1 head-to-head stalemate underscores the draw's appeal, with no major injuries altering lineups amid both teams' mid-table table positioning and playoff irrelevance.

markets.question: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Félix Tshisekedi ceases to be President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Félix Tshisekedi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Félix Tshisekedi and the government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Félix Tshisekedi's recent May 6 press conference, where he expressed openness to a third term via referendum if voters approve and warned that eastern DRC conflict with Rwanda-backed M23 rebels could delay 2028 presidential elections, has solidified trader consensus at 91.5% against his removal by year-end. Midway through his second constitutional term ending in 2028, Tshisekedi maintains firm control of Kinshasa and key institutions despite opposition outcry from figures like Martin Fayulu accusing him of power-clinging. No verified coups, impeachments, or resignations have emerged in the past 30 days, with ongoing eastern insecurity failing to threaten national leadership. Traders price in low risk of upheaval barring major scandals, military reversals, or health events before December 31.

markets.question: Will Josh Elliott win the 2026 Connecticut Governor Democratic primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Connecticut Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Gov. Ned Lamont's commanding position in the Connecticut Democratic gubernatorial primary, reflected in trader consensus at 94.5% odds, stems from his high approval ratings around 63% and dominant polling leads over challenger State Rep. Josh Elliott, including a February survey showing 57%-13% among likely primary voters and April data confirming his edge. As a popular two-term governor seeking a third, Lamont benefits from incumbency advantages, strong party support, and no other major contenders in the August 11 primary. Elliott's progressive campaign has gained modest name recognition by critiquing Lamont from the left, but lacks traction. The upcoming state Democratic convention this weekend offers Elliott a bid for 15% delegate support to force a contested endorsement; realistic disruptions would require a scandal, health issue, or surprise convention upset for Lamont.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the highest IPO Market Cap 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the company that achieves the highest market capitalization in U.S. dollars based on the official closing price on its first trading day in 2026.

This market will resolve to a company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If two or more companies have exactly equal highest closing market capitalizations, this market will resolve to the company whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In case the respective company's primary exchange’s official listing page does not report in U.S. dollars, it will be converted to U.S. dollars using the U.S. Federal Reserve Board’s H.10 foreign exchange reference rate for the relevant currency pair on the company’s first trading day (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/). If no such rate is available for the relevant trading day, the most recent previously published rate will be used. If the relevant currency is not listed, another credible exchange rate source will be used.

A listed company may resolve to "No" as soon as it becomes unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX dominates trader consensus at 86.5% implied probability for 2026's largest IPO by market cap, propelled by its early April confidential SEC filing targeting a June debut at a record $1.75-2 trillion valuation, amplified by February's all-stock merger with xAI that integrates cutting-edge AI models into Starlink's satellite network and ambitious multi-planetary goals, plus a fresh $55 billion Terafab chip facility filing for advanced compute. xAI holds 25.5% odds reflecting merger synergies but standalone doubts, while Anthropic's October IPO targeting sub-trillion scale lags amid slower timelines. OpenAI's high-valuation fundraising trails without firm listing signals; June roadshow and regulatory reviews loom as key catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ilia Topuria's overwhelming 77.5% implied probability stems from his status as reigning UFC lightweight champion after a first-round knockout of former titleholder Charles Oliveira at UFC 317 late last year, capping his undefeated run from featherweight with elite striking power and grappling control. Traders see him retaining through 2026, bolstered by his pound-for-pound ranking and no major injury setbacks despite a brief early-year hiatus; his next title defense against Justin Gaethje headlines UFC's June 14 White House event, pricing Gaethje at 12% on knockout threat and recent interim title win over Paddy Pimblett. Charles Oliveira (10.7%) and Arman Tsarukyan (9.5%) reflect contender momentum—Oliveira's resilience post-loss and Tsarukyan's top-ranked wins—while Benoît Saint Denis (7.1%) gains from submission upside, amid Islam Makhachev's welterweight move vacating the division.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Shanghai be 17°C on May 16?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 16 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecast ensembles from global models like GFS and ECMWF, as reflected in major aggregators, converge on a Shanghai minimum temperature of 19–20°C on May 16 under mostly sunny conditions with light southerly winds around 10–15 km/h. This trader consensus—implied probabilities tightly clustered at 30% for 20°C, 23% for 19°C, and 19.5% for 18°C—stems from a recent warm high-pressure ridge over eastern China following cooler early-May nights (13–19°C lows), limiting nocturnal radiative cooling in the urban heat island environment. Differentiating factors include cloud cover variability, which could suppress cooling to 21°C if overcast, or enhance it to 18°C under clear skies; urban effects and station-specific measurements (e.g., Shanghai Hongqiao) add 1–2°C uncertainty. Watch daily CMA updates and 00Z model runs for shifts ahead of resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Alexander Albon be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice, sitting 15th in Ligue 1 with 31 points after a 1-1 home draw against Lens in matchweek 32, face a critical home clash against relegated bottom-side FC Metz on May 17 at Allianz Riviera. Desperate to secure survival and avoid the relegation playoff—especially ahead of their pivotal away trip to 16th-placed Auxerre—the hosts hold a strong historical edge, unbeaten in recent home fixtures versus Metz and boasting just two losses in 19 such encounters. However, injuries plague Nice, with forward Elye Wahi (thigh), defender Moïse Bombito (lower leg), and others sidelined, mirroring Metz's extensive absences including Habib Diallo (hamstring) and multiple defenders. Metz's poor away form and demotivation post-relegation could favor a low-scoring affair, though Nice's leaky defense (58 goals conceded) adds upset risk.

markets.question: Will Ubisoft announce bankruptcy by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ubisoft announces that it will file for bankruptcy or has filed for bankruptcy of any variety by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement will suffice for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of if or when the actual filing occurs.

The announcement must be made through any of their official or verified channels, as a recorded or written statement by their CEO, legal representation, or other individual or team which officially represents Ubisoft.

A definitive consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Ubisoft announce bankruptcy by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ubisoft announces that it will file for bankruptcy or has filed for bankruptcy of any variety by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement will suffice for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of if or when the actual filing occurs.

The announcement must be made through any of their official or verified channels, as a recorded or written statement by their CEO, legal representation, or other individual or team which officially represents Ubisoft.

A definitive consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ubisoft's projected cash reserves of 1.25-1.35 billion euros by fiscal year-end March 2026—fully available to service debt maturities—drive the 97.5% trader consensus against a bankruptcy announcement by June 30, reflecting skin-in-the-game confidence in the game publisher's liquidity. January's sweeping restructuring, including cancellation of six underperforming titles and €500 million in targeted cost savings, addressed portfolio inefficiencies, while February's Q3 net bookings of €338 million beat expectations, fueled by strong Assassin's Creed franchise performance and licensing deals. Despite depressed share prices and projected €1 billion operating loss for FY2025-26, no verified insolvency signals have emerged; full-year earnings on May 20 remain a key catalyst, with severe Q4 shortfalls or refinancing snags as rare tail risks.

markets.question: Will The Greens win the second most seats in the 2026 Sachsen-Anhalt parliamentary elections?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt are scheduled to take place on September 6, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the second greatest number of seats in the state parliament of Sachsen-Anhalt (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Sachsen-Anhalt election for the Landtag does not occur by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid second votes received (Zweitstimme), with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published on the official website of the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt (https://www.landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Infratest dimap and MDR polls from early May 2026 show the AfD extending its lead to 41% in Sachsen-Anhalt Landtagswahl projections, with the CDU at 26%, solidifying trader consensus that the CDU will secure second place ahead of the September 6 election under proportional representation. The AfD's surge reflects strong eastern German support amid dissatisfaction with the federal coalition's handling of migration, economy, and energy costs, while the incumbent CDU-SPD-Greens government faces declining turnout risks for smaller parties like BSW (around 5%), FDP (3%), Greens (4%), SPD (6%), and The Left (7%), all polling below levels needed to challenge for second. No major campaign events have shifted dynamics in the past week, but continued AfD momentum could widen the gap further.

markets.question: Spread: CF Cruz Azul (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Liga MX game, scheduled for May 13 at 10:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CF Cruz Azul" if CF Cruz Azul win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CD Guadalajara".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligamx.net. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CF Cruz Azul vs. CD Guadalajara - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for May 13 at 10:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Liga MX Clausura 2026 semifinals first leg at Estadio Banorte, Cruz Azul hosts Chivas Guadalajara in the Clásico Nacional, with La Máquina holding a dominant recent head-to-head record—unbeaten in their last seven meetings (six wins, one draw)—bolstered by strong playoff form including a 3-2 aggregate quarterfinal win over Atlas. Chivas, second in the regular-season table, advanced past Tigres but face a key absence with Daniel Aguirre sidelined by injury, while Leonardo Sepúlveda remains doubtful; Cruz Azul could miss striker Nicolás Ibáñez due to a lingering muscular issue, though he may return for the second leg at Estadio Jalisco. Both squads prioritize clean sheets in this high-stakes rivalry, where home/away splits and rest advantages could sway trader consensus amid tight table positioning and liguilla implications.
